title: campanula jaubertiana subsp. andorrana
intro: Campanula jaubertiana subsp. andorrana, also known as the Andorran bellflower, is a subspecies of the Campanula jaubertiana plant, which is a flowering plant in the bellflower family. It is a perennial herb with blue-purple flowers, commonly used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions, thanks to its health benefits in reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. The herbal preparations of Campanula jaubertiana subsp. andorrana include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
